title: Pancreatic NET Tail Resection — PTH-Independent Hypercalcemia Cured Post-Op
subtitle: A Resident Training Module: ectopic 1α-hydroxylase from pNET (1,25-D 154), FDG + Ga-68 DOTANOC staging, immediate normocalcemia after resection, CgA surveillance baseline, prior AKI, and hypertensive urgency (194/102) at follow-up
authors: Resident/fellowship training module — illustrative teaching case (de-identified)
sourceFile: user-provided electronic health record summary (chat); patient name anonymized (+2 alphabet cipher)

relatedChapters.title: Severe PTH-Independent Hypercalcemia with Elevated Calcitriol
Refractory PTH-Independent Hypercalcemia in Metastatic Breast Cancer

patient.chiefComplaint: Post-operative follow-up after pancreatic tail mass resection; prior PTH-independent hypercalcemia with AKI.
pmh: Pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor (pNET) of the tail — resected; histopathology confirmed NET
PTH-independent hypercalcemia with elevated 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D (154) — resolved post-operatively
Acute kidney injury during hypercalcemic presentation — resolution pending confirmation
Hypertension (since 2000) — currently severely uncontrolled at follow-up
Bilateral osteoarthritis, frozen shoulder, cervical and lumbar spondylosis
Normal left ventricular function on prior evaluation

caseDescription:
Mr. Mocvc Rtcucf, a 70-year-old man (76 kg), re-presents for post-operative follow-up after surgical resection of a pancreatic tail mass. Pre-operatively he had PTH-independent hypercalcemia complicated by acute kidney injury. Key preoperative biochemistry included markedly elevated 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D at 154, pointing to ectopic 1α-hydroxylase activity as the driver of humoral hypercalcemia rather than primary hyperparathyroidism.

Imaging: FDG PET showed a hypermetabolic, relatively well-defined mass in the pancreatic tail without other malignant hypermetabolic foci. Ga-68 DOTANOC PET-CT confirmed a somatostatin-receptor-expressing soft-tissue mass in the pancreatic tail and identified a faint DOTANOC-avid small paraaortic lymph node suspicious for metastasis; no osteolytic skeletal lesions or structural pheochromocytomas were seen.

He underwent resection; histopathology confirmed neuroendocrine tumor. Serum calcium normalized immediately after tumor removal, confirming the pNET as the hypercalcemia source.

At current follow-up he is normocalcemic but hypertensive to 194/102 mmHg (pulse 76, RR 20, SpO₂ 98%) — a fluctuating pattern noted previously. Plan: KFT to monitor prior AKI resolution, ionized calcium for post-operative surveillance baseline, chromogranin A as baseline tumor marker, aggressive management of hypertensive urgency, and clinic review with pending reports.
keyFindings: FDG-avid pancreatic tail mass; Ga-68 DOTANOC confirms SSTR-expressing NET
Faint DOTANOC-avid paraaortic lymph node — suspicious nodal disease; upstages surveillance intensity
Immediate post-operative normocalcemia — curative resection of humoral hypercalcemia source
Prior AKI during hypercalcemia — requires KFT at follow-up
Severe uncontrolled hypertension 194/102 at revisit — hypertensive urgency separate from cured hypercalcemia
Chromogranin A ordered as post-resection baseline for future NET surveillance
finalDiagnosis: Resected well-differentiated pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor of the tail that caused PTH-independent, calcitriol-mediated hypercalcemia via ectopic 1α-hydroxylase — biochemically cured by resection with immediate normocalcemia. Possible regional nodal involvement (faint paraaortic DOTANOC-avid node) warrants oncologic staging review and structured NET surveillance (ionized calcium, chromogranin A, cross-sectional imaging per guidelines). Concurrent hypertensive urgency (194/102) and prior AKI require immediate BP management and renal function reassessment — not deferrable until surveillance labs return.
learningObjectives: Interpret PTH-independent hypercalcemia with elevated 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D as ectopic calcitriol production and localize the source.
Compare FDG PET and Ga-68 DOTANOC PET-CT roles in pancreatic NET diagnosis, receptor profiling, and nodal staging.
Explain how immediate post-operative normocalcemia confirms tumor-driven humoral hypercalcemia.
Design post-resection NET surveillance: ionized calcium, chromogranin A baseline, and imaging follow-up.
Recognize and urgently manage severe hypertension at a post-operative visit without conflating it with the resolved hypercalcemia.

items.title: Why does 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D of 154 define PTH-independent, calcitriol-mediated hypercalcemia in this case
items.content:
In hypercalcemia work-up, intact PTH is the first branch point. Suppressed PTH with elevated calcium defines PTH-independent disease. Among PTH-independent causes, the 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D (calcitriol) level separates mechanisms:

• Elevated 1,25-D with normal/moderate 25-OH-D → unregulated extra-renal 1α-hydroxylase (CYP27B1) activity in tumor or granuloma macrophages — not vitamin D intoxication and not primary hyperparathyroidism.

Mr. Mocvc Rtcucf's preoperative 1,25-D of 154 (with PTH-independent hypercalcemia) points to ectopic calcitriol production. The clinical trap is to treat this as 'vitamin D excess' or to chase primary hyperparathyroidism when PTH is already suppressed.

Compare cr-003 and cr-010 for other calcitriol-mediated hypercalcemia patterns (granulomatous vs dual-mechanism malignancy).
keyPoints: Suppressed PTH + high Ca = PTH-independent
High 1,25-D + non-toxic 25-OH-D → ectopic 1α-hydroxylase
1,25-D 154 here localizes mechanism before anatomy

items.title: How should PTH-independent hypercalcemia be worked up stepwise before surgery in this patient
items.content:
Stepwise panel on presentation:
1. Confirm true hypercalcemia — ionized or albumin-corrected calcium.
2. Intact PTH — expect suppression in tumor-driven calcitriol states.
3. 25-hydroxyvitamin D and 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D — pattern here (elevated 1,25-D) mandates search for CYP27B1-expressing tissue.
4. PTHrP — exclude humoral hypercalcemia of malignancy (bone resorption pathway); may coexist in some cancers but was not the leading story once 1,25-D dominated.
5. Renal function — hypercalcemia caused AKI in this patient; creatinine/eGFR track perfusion and nephrocalcinosis risk.
6. Cross-sectional imaging + functional PET — localize secreting mass.

Pitfall: starting bisphosphonate or denosumab without locating the source delays curative resection. In a resectable pNET, surgery is definitive.
keyPoints: PTH, 25-OH-D, 1,25-D, PTHrP, renal function — core panel
Elevated 1,25-D → image for CYP27B1-expressing lesion
Do not anchor on bisphosphonate alone if curative surgery is possible

items.title: Why can a pancreatic NET cause humoral hypercalcemia without being a 'functional' insulinoma or gastrinoma
items.content:
Functional NET syndromes classically refer to hormone excess with characteristic clinical syndromes (insulin, gastrin, VIP, glucagon). Hypercalcemia from NET is a paraneoplastic phenomenon: tumor cells (or associated stromal macrophages) express 1α-hydroxylase and convert 25-OH-D to active 1,25-(OH)₂-D, driving intestinal calcium absorption and osteoclast-mediated bone resorption.

This pNET presented with life-threatening calcium disturbance rather than hypoglycemia or peptic ulcer disease. The teaching point: always measure calcium in unexplained AKI or confusion even when the obvious narrative is 'pancreatic mass' — and always send 1,25-D when PTH is suppressed.

Rare but documented; granulomatous disease remains the more common 1,25-D mechanism — imaging distinguished a pancreatic tail neoplasm here.
keyPoints: NET hypercalcemia = paraneoplastic calcitriol, not classic functional syndrome
1α-hydroxylase expression in tumor tissue drives 1,25-D elevation
Do not limit NET work-up to insulin/gastrin alone

items.title: How do FDG PET and Ga-68 DOTANOC PET-CT complement each other in this pancreatic tail mass
items.content:
FDG PET in this case: hypermetabolic, relatively well-defined pancreatic tail mass — supports malignancy/neoplastic biology and showed no other malignant hypermetabolic foci (reassuring against widespread metastatic disease on glucose metabolism imaging).

Ga-68 DOTANOC PET-CT: confirmed somatostatin receptor (SSTR) expression on the primary — defining the lesion as NET-family and guiding receptor-targeted therapy options (somatostatin analogs, PRRT) if needed.

Critical addition from DOTANOC: faint avidity in a small paraaortic lymph node — suspicious for nodal metastasis even when FDG showed no other foci. DOTANOC is often more sensitive for well-differentiated NET nodal disease because SSTR density can exceed FDG avidity in low-proliferation NETs.

Pitfall: stopping staging at FDG alone and missing SSTR-positive nodal disease.
keyPoints: FDG: metabolic activity, excludes widespread FDG-avid metastases here
DOTANOC: SSTR expression — confirms NET phenotype + nodal staging
Faint paraaortic DOTANOC node may be FDG-subtle but clinically important

items.title: Why does a faint DOTANOC-avid paraaortic lymph node change post-operative management even after curative-intent resection
items.content:
Immediate normocalcemia after resection proves the primary tumor was the calcitriol source — but does not prove node-negative disease. The faint DOTANOC-expressing paraaortic node flagged pre-operatively remains a staging uncertainty: micrometastasis vs reactive node.

This changes surveillance intensity: closer interval imaging, baseline chromogranin A (CgA) for trend monitoring, and multidisciplinary review of whether adjuvant somatostatin analog therapy is indicated per grade, Ki-67, and ENETS/WHO staging once final pathology (including nodal harvest) is integrated.

Trap: declaring 'cured' at the first normocalcemic visit without reconciling pre-op nodal PET findings with operative pathology and post-op CgA.
keyPoints: Normocalcemia ≠ automatic node-negative cure
Pre-op DOTANOC node → higher surveillance tier
Integrate pathology nodal yield with PET findings

items.title: How does immediate post-operative normocalcemia confirm the pNET as the hypercalcemia source
items.content:
Humoral hypercalcemia from ectopic calcitriol resolves when the secreting tissue is removed — analogous to curing primary hyperparathyroidism after parathyroidectomy.

Mr. Mocvc Rtcucf's calcium normalized immediately after pancreatic tail resection. This natural experiment is stronger than any preoperative inference: the tumor was necessary and sufficient for the hypercalcemic state.

Residual monitoring still matters — ectopic tissue at nodal sites could theoretically persist — but the acute biochemical cure shifts priority from calcium lowering drugs to NET oncology surveillance and managing comorbidities (hypertension, diabetes, COPD).
keyPoints: Immediate Ca normalization post-resection = proof of tumor-driven mechanism
Natural experiment confirming ectopic 1α-hydroxylase source
Shift focus to surveillance and comorbidities after biochemical cure

items.title: Why did hypercalcemia cause AKI and why must kidney function be rechecked at this follow-up
items.content:
Hypercalcemia reduces renal perfusion (vasoconstriction, dehydration from nephrogenic DI) and causes intrarenal calcium deposition — both precipitate acute kidney injury, especially in elderly patients with hypertension and diabetes.

This patient presented with AKI during the hypercalcemic illness. Now normocalcemic, renal recovery is expected but not guaranteed — particularly if tubular injury or nephrocalcinosis occurred.

KFT at follow-up is mandatory: creatinine, eGFR, electrolytes. Pitfall: attributing all renal concern to 'resolved calcium' without confirming creatinine trend. Persistent CKD changes long-term surveillance intervals and drug dosing (SSA, antihypertensives).
keyPoints: Hypercalcemia → vasoconstriction, dehydration, nephrocalcinosis → AKI
Normocalcemia permits recovery but does not prove normalized GFR
Repeat KFT at post-op follow-up — non-negotiable

items.title: How should ionized calcium be used in post-operative surveillance after calcitriol-mediated NET hypercalcemia
items.content:
Total serum calcium is influenced by albumin; ionized calcium is the physiologically active fraction and the best surveillance metric after tumor-driven hypercalcemia.

Order ionized calcium at baseline post-resection (this visit) and at guideline-defined intervals — earlier if symptoms (confusion, polyuria, constipation) recur.

Recurrent hypercalcemia after resection raises concern for nodal/local recurrence or occult metastasis (the faint DOTANOC paraaortic node) — not 'idiopathic' relapse. Pair rising calcium with CgA and cross-sectional/SSTR imaging.
keyPoints: Ionized calcium > total calcium for surveillance accuracy
Baseline post-op level establishes new reference
Recurrent hypercalcemia → search for NET recurrence, not repeat PTH work-up first

items.title: Why is chromogranin A ordered as a baseline tumor marker at this post-operative visit
items.content:
Chromogranin A (CgA) is co-secreted from neuroendocrine granules and serves as a general NET surveillance biomarker — not hormone-specific but useful for trend monitoring when interpreted with confounders (PPI use, renal impairment, hypertension) in mind.

This post-resection sample establishes the patient's new baseline ('new normal') after tumor removal. Future rises from baseline prompt imaging before symptoms.

See ht-006 for Bayesian interpretation: moderate sensitivity, high specificity; a single value matters less than the trend. In this patient with prior AKI and hypertension, interpret CgA alongside renal function and PPI exposure.
keyPoints: Post-op CgA = baseline for future trend monitoring
Not diagnostic alone — surveillance biomarker
Confounders: PPI, renal dysfunction, BP meds — see ht-006

items.title: Why is blood pressure 194/102 at follow-up an immediate problem separate from the cured hypercalcemia
items.content:
The patient is now normocalcemic — the NET-related emergency is biochemically resolved — but BP 194/102 mmHg defines hypertensive urgency (severe elevation without necessarily acute end-organ damage yet, but high short-term risk).

He has long-standing hypertension since 2000, T2DM, and age 70 — high ASCVD risk. Prior notes describe fluctuating BP; today's reading cannot be dismissed as 'white coat' without verification and treatment.

Trap: focusing the visit entirely on 'great news — calcium normal' while sending a hypertensive urgency home untreated. Immediate management is warranted: confirm reading, assess symptoms (chest pain, dyspnea, neuro deficit), optimize/combine antihypertensives, and arrange close re-check — balancing COPD and diabetes medication interactions.
keyPoints: 194/102 = hypertensive urgency — treat now, not at next routine visit
Resolved hypercalcemia does not mitigate stroke/MI risk from severe HTN
Fluctuating BP history — verify, treat, close follow-up

items.title: How should hypertensive urgency be approached in a 70-year-old with T2DM, COPD, and recent major surgery
items.content:
Stepwise approach at this visit:
1. Repeat BP in both arms after 5 minutes rest; confirm no pain/anxiety contributor.
2. Screen for end-organ symptoms — chest pain, dyspnea, focal neuro deficit, visual change, AKI.
3. Review current antihypertensives — adherence, dosing, drug interactions (avoid abrupt β-blocker withdrawal).
4. Pharmacologic lowering: reinstate or uptitrate home regimen if adherent; if untreated or subtherapeutic, initiate/adjust agents compatible with COPD (generally avoid non-selective β-blockade in reactive airway disease unless compelling indication; consider ACEi/ARB + CCB ± thiazide-like diuretic per guidelines).
5. Avoid aggressive IV lowering unless true emergency with end-organ damage — urgency permits oral titration over hours with reassessment.
6. Coordinate with diabetes management — many agents are kidney-protective and overlap with BP goals.

Document plan and arrange 24–72 hour BP recheck.
keyPoints: Confirm BP, assess end-organ symptoms first
Oral titration appropriate for urgency without acute damage
COPD: cautious β-blocker choice; ACEi/ARB + CCB often suitable

items.title: How do T2DM, COPD, and musculoskeletal disease shape long-term NET follow-up in this patient
items.content:
T2DM: influences surgical recovery, infection risk, and choice/timing of somatostatin analogs (which can worsen glycemia). Glucose monitoring should intensify if SSA therapy is considered for nodal disease.

COPD: limits β-blocker options for hypertension; emphasizes inhaled bronchodilator optimization and smoking cessation if applicable. Stress perioperative steroid use if given — may transiently worsen glucose.

Bilateral OA, frozen shoulder, cervical/lumbar spondylosis: affect mobility and quality of life but also complicate imaging interpretation (degenerative uptake on PET). Do not attribute DOTANOC nodal avidity to spine disease without anatomic correlation.

Normal LV function is reassuring for aggressive BP management and future oncologic therapies.
keyPoints: T2DM + SSA therapy → monitor glucose closely
COPD shapes antihypertensive and perioperative choices
Degenerative spine disease — correlate PET nodes anatomically

items.title: Post-resection follow-up checklist for this pNET with prior calcitriol-mediated hypercalcemia
items.content:
At this visit (ordered in case):
☐ KFT — AKI resolution/CKD staging
☐ Ionized calcium — post-op baseline
☐ Chromogranin A — post-op baseline (note PPI/renal confounders)

Pending integration:
☐ Final surgical pathology — grade, Ki-67, margins, lymph nodes harvested vs DOTANOC-positive paraaortic node
☐ MDT oncology review — adjuvant SSA vs observation
☐ Structured imaging follow-up per ENETS (typically cross-sectional ± SSTR PET at intervals based on stage/grade)

Same visit urgent actions:
☐ Treat hypertensive urgency 194/102
☐ Medication reconciliation (antihypertensives, diabetes, COPD inhalers)
☐ Patient education — symptoms of recurrence (hypercalcemia, flushing, hypoglycemia) and when to seek care
keyPoints: Labs: KFT, ionized Ca, CgA baseline this visit
Pathology + DOTANOC node → adjuvant therapy decision
Hypertensive urgency — treat today

items.question: Why is elevated 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D with suppressed PTH the biochemical signature of ectopic calcitriol production rather than primary hyperparathyroidism?
items.answer: Primary hyperparathyroidism drives hypercalcemia through PTH-mediated bone resorption, renal calcium reabsorption, and renal 1α-hydroxylase — so PTH is elevated or inappropriately normal, not suppressed. When PTH is suppressed, the parathyroid glands are responding appropriately to high calcium. Elevated 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D in that setting means calcitriol is being produced outside the normal renal feedback loop — by tumor or granuloma 1α-hydroxylase (CYP27B1). In Mr. Mocvc Rtcucf, 1,25-D of 154 with PTH-independent hypercalcemia localized the mechanism to ectopic hydroxylase activity, which imaging then attributed to a pancreatic tail NET.
keyPoints: Primary HPT: high/normal PTH, not suppressed
Suppressed PTH + high 1,25-D = ectopic CYP27B1
Mechanism guided imaging to pNET

items.question: How should Ga-68 DOTANOC PET-CT findings change surgical and post-operative planning compared with FDG PET alone?
items.answer: FDG PET confirmed a hypermetabolic pancreatic tail mass and no other FDG-avid metastases — supporting neoplastic biology. DOTANOC added two decisive elements: (1) somatostatin receptor expression, confirming NET phenotype and opening somatostatin-analog/PRRT pathways if needed; (2) detection of a faint DOTANOC-avid paraaortic lymph node suspicious for metastasis — potentially upstaging disease and influencing extent of lymphadenectomy and adjuvant therapy. Surgery proceeded with curative intent on the primary; post-operatively the DOTANOC node mandates pathology correlation and intensified surveillance even though calcium normalized immediately.
keyPoints: DOTANOC confirms SSTR+ NET biology
Nodal DOTANOC avidity → staging/surveillance upgrade
FDG alone would miss SSTR-dominant nodal disease risk

items.stem: Mr. Mocvc Rtcucf, 70, had hypercalcemia with suppressed PTH and 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D 154 before surgery. Which mechanism is most likely?
options: Primary hyperparathyroidism with secondary vitamin D elevation
Ectopic 1α-hydroxylase activity producing calcitriol
Vitamin D intoxication from exogenous supplementation only
Familial hypocalciuric hypercalcemia
items.explanation: Suppressed PTH excludes primary HPT. Markedly elevated 1,25-D indicates ectopic calcitriol production — here from pNET. Vitamin D intoxication typically shows very high 25-OH-D with suppressed 1,25-D via feedback.

items.stem: FDG PET shows a hypermetabolic pancreatic tail mass without other FDG-avid lesions. Ga-68 DOTANOC confirms SSTR expression in the mass and faint avidity in a paraaortic lymph node. Best synthesis?
options: Benign pancreatic cyst — observe
SSTR-expressing pancreatic NET with possible nodal involvement
Pheochromocytoma — start alpha-blockade immediately
FDG-negative disease rules out malignancy
items.explanation: FDG-avid pancreatic mass + DOTANOC SSTR expression = NET. Faint nodal DOTANOC suggests possible metastasis. No pheochromocytoma was seen structurally.

items.stem: Calcium normalizes immediately after resection of the pancreatic tail NET. What does this confirm?
options: The patient never had true hypercalcemia
The resected tumor was the source of calcitriol-mediated hypercalcemia
Primary hyperparathyroidism was cured incidentally
Post-operative hypoparathyroidism is the cause of normal calcium
items.explanation: Immediate normocalcemia after tumor removal proves humoral hypercalcemia was tumor-driven via ectopic 1α-hydroxylase — a natural therapeutic experiment.

items.stem: At post-operative follow-up for this resected pNET, which investigations are appropriately ordered per the case plan?
options: PTH level only — to screen for parathyroid adenoma
KFT, ionized calcium, and chromogranin A
24-hour urinary calcium only
Serum ACE and 1,25-D weekly for life
items.explanation: KFT monitors prior AKI resolution; ionized calcium surveils recurrence of calcitriol-mediated hypercalcemia; CgA establishes NET surveillance baseline.

items.stem: Mr. Mocvc Rtcucf is normocalcemic at follow-up but BP is 194/102 mmHg without acute end-organ symptoms. Best immediate management?
options: Discharge — calcium is normal, BP can wait 6 months
Aggressive management of hypertensive urgency with reassessment within 24–72 hours
Emergency PCI for presumed ACS regardless of symptoms
Stop all antihypertensives to avoid hypotension after surgery
items.explanation: 194/102 is hypertensive urgency — requires prompt oral therapy and close follow-up despite resolved hypercalcemia. Not a hypertensive emergency unless end-organ damage present.

items.stem: A faint DOTANOC-avid paraaortic node was seen pre-operatively. Post-op calcium is normal and histology confirms NET. Next best conceptual step?
options: Ignore the node — calcium cure proves no metastasis
Correlate operative nodal pathology with PET, establish CgA baseline, and plan guideline-based NET surveillance
Start platinum chemotherapy immediately for all NETs
Repeat DOTANOC only if calcium rises — no baseline CgA needed
items.explanation: Biochemical cure of hypercalcemia does not exclude nodal metastasis. Integrate pathology, baseline CgA, and structured surveillance; adjuvant SSA per stage/grade at MDT.

items.statement: Immediate normalization of serum calcium after pancreatic tail NET resection in this case supports the tumor as the source of PTH-independent hypercalcemia.
items.explanation: Removal of the secreting tumor abruptly stops ectopic calcitriol production — calcium normalizes immediately, confirming causation.

items.statement: A negative FDG PET for extra-pancreatic lesions eliminates the need for Ga-68 DOTANOC PET-CT in well-differentiated pancreatic NET staging.
items.explanation: Well-differentiated NETs may be SSTR-positive with faint or absent FDG nodal uptake. DOTANOC identified a suspicious paraaortic node FDG alone might underestimate.

items.assertion: Suppressed PTH with elevated 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D in this patient indicates ectopic calcitriol production.
items.reason: When calcium is high, normal parathyroid glands suppress PTH; if 1,25-D remains elevated, calcitriol is being synthesized outside renal feedback control.
items.explanation: Both true and causally linked — the biochemical pattern uniquely points to ectopic 1α-hydroxylase, here in pNET tissue.

items.assertion: Because hypercalcemia has resolved, severe hypertension at follow-up can be deferred to the next routine appointment.
items.reason: Normocalcemia eliminates all cardiovascular risk in elderly diabetic patients.
items.explanation: Both false — hypertensive urgency (194/102) requires immediate management; normocalcemia does not eliminate ASCVD/stroke risk, especially with T2DM and longstanding HTN.

items.question: Name three laboratory tests ordered at this post-operative follow-up and state the purpose of each.
items.answer: KFT (monitor resolution of prior AKI and adjust drug dosing); ionized calcium (baseline surveillance for recurrence of tumor-driven hypercalcemia); chromogranin A (baseline NET tumor marker for future trend monitoring).

items.question: List two urgent priorities at this visit beyond waiting for pending laboratory reports.
items.answer: Aggressive management of hypertensive urgency (BP 194/102) with confirmation, symptom screen, and antihypertensive adjustment; clinical review plan integrating surgical pathology with pre-operative DOTANOC nodal findings for NET surveillance/adjuvant therapy decisions.
